description: Evaluation suite for deploy/skills/ — validates structured JSON output and key field presence

evals.name: flags unverified claims as NEEDS WORK
evals.input: The API is working correctly and all endpoints return proper status codes.

evals.name: returns SHIP IT when evidence is provided
evals.input: Test suite ran: 47 passed, 0 failed. Auth integration test covers login, token refresh, and logout. cargo audit reports no open vulnerabilities.

evals.name: returns BLOCKED with grade F when no evidence provided
evals.input: Done.

evals.name: produces structured output for a feature request
evals.input: Add a function isPrime(n: number): boolean to a TypeScript utility file.

evals.name: identifies security issue in code review
evals.input: Review this Express handler: app.get('/user', (req, res) => { db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ' + req.query.id).then(r => res.json(r)) })

evals.name: produces data model before API endpoints
evals.input: Design a simple URL shortener service. Users can create short links and get redirect counts.

evals.name: includes auth on every endpoint
evals.input: Design a multi-tenant project management API with workspaces and tasks.

evals.name: decomposes PR review into parallel first-pass tasks
evals.input: Review a pull request: check code quality, security issues, and test coverage.

evals.name: returns single-phase plan for simple task
evals.input: Generate unit tests for a single pure function.

evals.name: produces runnable GitHub Actions config for Rust project
evals.input: Create a GitHub Actions CI pipeline for a Rust project: build, test, clippy, fmt check.

evals.name: lists manual steps that cannot be automated
evals.input: Generate a Dockerfile and deployment script for a Node.js app that connects to a Postgres database.

evals.name: identifies SQL injection as CRITICAL
evals.input: Review: app.get('/user', (req, res) => { db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = ' + req.query.id) })

evals.name: does not invent findings when code is clean
evals.input: Review: const userId = req.params.id; const user = await db.findById(userId);

evals.name: generates auth failure test case for every authenticated endpoint
evals.input: Generate tests for POST /api/jobs — requires bearer auth, returns {id, status: 'queued'}.

evals.name: includes at least one validation error test
evals.input: Generate tests for POST /api/users with required fields: email (valid format), name (non-empty).

evals.name: identifies missing SLA as a procurement blocker
evals.input: kx is an open source CLI dev assistant. MIT license. No enterprise support tier.

evals.name: evaluates onboarding friction
evals.input: kx installs via cargo install. First run: kx dev. Requires ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var.

evals.name: flags data exfiltration risk for tool sending prompts to external APIs
evals.input: kx sends developer prompts to third-party AI provider APIs (Anthropic, OpenAI, etc.).

evals.name: translates technical description into plain business language
evals.input: kx is a Rust CLI that wraps LLM provider APIs with a REPL interface and automatic stack detection.

evals.name: reports failure when reality-checker returns BLOCKED
evals.input: workflow: [{id: 'validate', skill: 'reality-checker', input: 'Done.'}]

evals.name: completes successfully when all steps pass
evals.input: workflow: [{id: 'design', skill: 'backend-architect', input: 'Design a health check endpoint'}, {id: 'validate', skill: 'reality-checker', input: 'output from design step', depends_on: ['design']}]

evals.name: flags Cognitive Load violation on overloaded dashboard
evals.input: Dashboard with 15 KPI cards, 4 filter dropdowns, a data table with 12 columns, and 3 sidebar action buttons all visible simultaneously on first load. No grouping or progressive disclosure.

evals.name: applies Part V AI principles to AI assistant interface
evals.input: AI writing assistant: generates text suggestions inline. No label indicating output is AI-generated. No confidence level shown. No way to dismiss or reject a suggestion without accepting it first.

evals.name: marks checkout form with 22 fields as launch blocker
evals.input: Checkout form with 22 fields: billing address (6), shipping address (6), card number, expiry, CVV, name on card, email, phone, promo code, gift message, newsletter opt-in, terms checkbox. All on one page. No inline validation. Submit button at bottom only.

evals.name: does not invent violations on a clean single-action screen
evals.input: Modal dialog with a single input field (email address), a clear label, inline validation on blur, a primary CTA button (44px height, full-width), and a visible close button. No other elements.

evals.name: scores Poor and lists critical blockers when all AI crawlers are blocked and schema is absent
evals.input: Site robots.txt disallows: Googlebot, gptbot, claudebot, perplexitybot, ccbot, anthropic-ai, chatgpt-user, bytespider. No JSON-LD on any page. Homepage is 320 words with one H1. No meta description. No sitemap.xml.

evals.name: scores Good or higher when crawlers allowed, schema present, content strong
evals.input: Site allows all AI crawlers in robots.txt (gptbot, claudebot, perplexitybot, google-extended, ccbot, chatgpt-user). Has Organization + SoftwareApplication JSON-LD with name, description, url, image, applicationCategory. Homepage is 1,400 words with H1, H2, H3 structure, 12 statistics with percentages, meta description 120 chars. Has sitemap.xml.

evals.name: generates SoftwareApplication + Organization schemas for a SaaS CLI tool
evals.input: kx is an open source CLI AI assistant for developers. MIT license. Free. Supports 11 AI providers including Claude, GPT-4, and Gemini. Built in Rust. GitHub: github.com/kernex-dev/kernex-agent. Version 0.2.0.

evals.name: refuses to generate placeholder schemas when no context is provided
evals.input: Generate schema for my website.
